sections.content:

- Red block at [24,18]-[29,21] disappeared (R -> c)
- Red block at [30,12]-[33,17] reappeared (c -> R)
So clicking the right-panel shape TOGGLES the stamp on/off. When I first clicked, it stamped the red block at [24,18]-[29,21]. When I clicked again, it removed that stamp and restored the original red block at [30,12]-[33,17].
This means the mechanic is a TOGGLE: clicking a shape on the right moves its corresponding block between two positions on the left panel.
Initial state:
- Red block at [30,12]-[33,17] (original position)
- Blue checkerboard at [20,18]-[23,21] (original position)
After clicking red once:
- Red block moved to [24,18]-[29,21] (new position)
- Original red at [30,12]-[33,17] disappeared
After clicking red again (toggle back):
- Red block back at [30,12]-[33,17]
- [24,18]-[29,21] cleared
Similarly for blue:
Initial: blue checkerboard at [20,18]-[23,21]
After clicking blue: blue checkerboard moved to [34,8]-[37,11]
So the puzzle is about moving blocks between positions by clicking the corresponding right-panel shape. The goal is probably to arrange the left panel in a specific configuration.

- Blue checkerboard at [34,8]-[37,11] (still in new position since I only clicked blue once)

- Below: red block (horizontal)

- Below: gray box with green

- Red is back at [30,12]-[33,17] (original position after toggle)

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Current left panel colored objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
    min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
    max_r = max(p[0] for p in b)
    max_c = max(p[1] for p in b)
    if max_c < 32 and n['color'] not in ['c', 'g', 'G']:
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, size={n['pixels']}, bbox=[{min_r},{min_c}]-[{max_r},{max_c}]")

# The goal is likely to have all blocks in their "stamped" positions
# Currently: red is at original [30,12]-[33,17], blue is at stamped [34,8]-[37,11]
# Need to click red to move it to stamped position [24,18]-[29,21]
# But first let's check if blue needs to be toggled back too

# Let's think about what the "solved" state might look like
# Initial state had:
# - Blue at [20,18]-[23,21]
# - Red at [30,12]-[33,17]
# - DarkRed at [30,18]-[33,21]
# Stamped positions:
# - Blue at [34,8]-[37,11]
# - Red at [24,18]-[29,21]

# The goal might be to have both in stamped positions
# Let's click red to stamp it
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 19, 'col': 48}])
print(f"\nAfter clicking red: board_changed={last_action_result.get('board_changed')}, level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")

seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
print("\nCurrent left panel colored objects after red click:")
for n in seg2['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
    min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
    max_r = max(p[0] for p in b)
    max_c = max(p[1] for p in b)
    if max_c < 32 and n['color'] not in ['c', 'g', 'G']:
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, size={n['pixels']}, bbox=[{min_r},{min_c}]-[{max_r},{max_c}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
